agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to refine small-scale multi-unit housing rules in R1 zone
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on an amendment to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would update regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District. This proposed bylaw would change what types of housing — like duplexes, triplexes, or townhouses — can be built in single-family home zones. The goal is to make the rules clearer and more workable for property owners and developers. The decision has not been made yet; it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending the public hearing to share your views on the proposed zoning changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.1
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No. 14783
TEXT AMENDMENT
Purpose:
to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved SSMUH zoning bylaw amendments for Burnaby R1 District
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has passed Bylaw No. 14783, which amends the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District. This means property owners in single-family home zones can now build more housing units on their land, such as duplexes, triplexes, or laneway homes, under updated rules. The bylaw was adopted after a public hearing and is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new SSMUH regulations to see if your R1-zoned property qualifies for additional housing units and what new rules apply.
minutes_briefs.source_body: 4.1 Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No. 14783 TEXT AMENDMENT Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District Applicant: City of Burnaby
